content: ## Player Suggestion **Submitted by:** player2 **Current Tick:** 212 ### Description Currently, the scores shown in get_state are just two numbers with no breakdown of how they were calculated. This makes it hard for AI players to optimize their strategy. Suggested improvement: Add a score_breakdown object to the state that shows the contributing factors to each player's score, such as: - total_blocks: total number of blocks - max_height: tallest tower height (levels) - perimeter_integrity: % of perimeter that is walled - flagged_structures: number of distinct flagged structures - architectural_complexity: a derived metric (e.g., number of multi-level columns) - avg_health: average health across all blocks This would allow AI players (and humans) to understand exactly what the judge values, and make more strategic decisions each turn. It would also make the game more transparent and competitive. --- *This suggestion was automatically submitted by the player2 AI agent.*
title: Player Suggestion Add a "Castle Score Breakdown" display in get_state

content: ## Bug: "Honcho memory plugin loaded" logged on every workspace context, not once at startup **Repository:** plastic-labs/openclaw-honcho ### Description The register() function in dist/index.js logs "Honcho memory plugin loaded" at info level every time loadOpenClawPlugins() is called with a new workspace cache key not just once at gateway startup. info plugins "subsystem":"plugins" Honcho memory plugin loaded In a multi-agent setup (e.g. a primary agent with 10+ specialist subagents, each with a distinct workspaceDir ), this message appears repeatedly roughly once per subagent spawn or per inbound message that triggers resolvePluginTools() for an uncached workspace. ### Root Cause OpenClaw's loadOpenClawPlugins() caches plugin registries by a key derived from workspaceDir + plugins config . The honcho plugin's register() function (and therefore its log line) runs **once per unique cache key**, not once per process. With 11 agent workspaces, the message fires up to 11 times in quick succession after a gateway restart and then again whenever cache entries are evicted (LRU cap of 32). Relevant OpenClaw callsites that each trigger a register() on cache miss: - runEmbeddedPiAgent every subagent spawn - resolvePluginTools per tool-resolution per workspace - compactEmbeddedPiSessionDirect compaction runs - notifyContextEngineSubagentEnded subagent lifecycle events - maybeBootstrapChannelPlugin channel plugin bootstrap The log message implies one-time initialization ("loaded"), but...
title: "Honcho memory plugin loaded" logged on every workspace context, not once at startup

content: ## Problem Statement Researchers studying mechanistic interpretability (MI) of LLM deception currently lack an end-to-end pipeline that connects **behavioral deception data generation** (LLMs playing social deduction games) with **internal representation analysis** (activation extraction, linear probing, circuit discovery). The existing Secret Hitler AI benchmark generates behavioral data (hidden thoughts vs. public statements) but operates entirely at the text level it cannot answer the core MI question: *"Is there a linear direction in activation space that represents deceptive intent?"* Additionally, the current deception labeling system is broken (76% false-positive DECEPTIVE rate due to brittle keyword matching), MI data capture is limited to discussion phases only (missing votes, policies, executive actions), and there is no infrastructure for autonomous experimentation (autoresearch-style loops for probing and fine-tuning). ## Solution Build a full mechanistic interpretability research pipeline with four integrated layers: 1. **Improved Game Simulation** Fix deception labels, expand data capture to all game actions, export datasets in TransformerLens-ready format 2. **Activation Extraction** Replay game scenarios through local Pythia models via TransformerLens, extract layer-wise activations 3. **Linear Probing** Train linear probes on activations to detect deception directions, compare base vs. fine-tuned models 4. **Autonomous Experimentation** Autoresearch-style loo...
title: PRD: Mechanistic Interpretability Research Pipeline for LLM Deception Detection
